gold_texts: ["Psoriasis.  Use orally Psoriasis capsules, glycyrrhizic acid glycosides, transfer factors.  Topically, use Momosone, tacrolimus.", "Psoriasis. .....", "Psoriasis Vulgaris.  Use chinese medicine for treatment.", "Looks like Psoriasis.", "Psoriasis Vulgaris", "Should be Psoriasis.", "kerion in chinese medicine.", "Psoriasis Vulgaris.  Is a genetic disease with multiple genes.  Prescribing Cabotriol, halomethasone for topical use.  Orally take Ke Yin Wan (A Chinese herbal drug), compound oxalate glycoside.  Prevent from infections (bacteria, virus, fungi).  Prevent from injury (bath with water below 40 degree C).  Getting Koebner Phenomenon.  Avoid consumption of alcohol.  Avoid drug of beta receptors blockers and NSAID drugs.", "Tinea.  Caused by fungal infection. Recommendation: 1.        Topical application of clotrimazole ointment, Miao natural ointment, Dakonin ointment or clotrimazole tinea solution.  Treatment time: 2 – 4 weeks. 2.      Orally use Ketoconazole or Griseofulvin.  Treatment time: 2 – 4 weeks.  Should supplement that with anti-inflammatory drugs.  As the infection could involve many sources,  use drugs such as floxacin, azithromycin, clindamycin, etc.  If having beri beri, should be dealt with timely to avoid cross infections.  Should use vitamins C, AD, E and B types vitamins, with small amount of zinc, iron, calcium etc.  3.      Avoid contact with outside  ** material and locally **.  Don’t scratch, don’t rub with force, don’t use hot water and soap when bathing.  Don’t consume alcohol, don’t drink coffee, don’t drink tea, don’t eat something sour, spicy or other ** food. 4.      Locally poke the needle knife point to dredge open the blood vessels (This is Chinese Acupuncture Medicine)."]

gold_texts: ["frictional lichenoid rash", "Could be frictional lichenoid rash or lichen striatus", "frictional lichenoid rash", "frictional lichenoid rash", "frictional lichenoid rash", "Also called sandy dermatitis or juvenile papular dermatitis. candidate: Likely chronic hand eczema, possibly irritant contact dermatitis. Patch testing recommended.  Avoid irritants.  See dermatologist.
query_text: Related to Eczema, pompholyx.  Request for diagnosis. Suffering eczema and epompholyx at hands from the year of 2010.  It is attacking me recurrently.  Very painful.  Hope that physicians from all places could take a look.  Following is the history:-  Work History:  Graduated in June, 2009.  Start working after that.  Work requires contact with  hydrochloric acid with low concentration inside a trench.  It is used to clean the oxidized surface of metal.  There is no use of protective gloves durig the process, and has direct contact with the hydrochloric acid.  Both hands are involved in the physical work, have direct contact with industrial grease.  Change of work place in March 2010.  Working in the office there, with occasional contact with metal.  Not sure it is caused by allergy with metal.  Symptoms:  Blisters on the sides of both palms.  Very itchy.  If broken after scratching, liquid of light yellow in color is flowing out.  The more I scratch, the more is the itching.  Scab is forming after lesions.  Skin turns hard and starts shredding.  Mostly happening in Summer;  less serious in Winter.  Medical History: 1.  Diagnosis as eczema and epompholyx at the Dermatology Department of a local hospital.  Treatment is:  soaking in diluted Aluminum acetate solution, and topical use of Triamcinolone Acetonide Acetate and Miconazole Nitrate Neomycin Sulfate Cream.  No improvement after using them. 2. Soaking in salicylic acid solution by myself.  Effect is not obvious.  Have tried that 5 times. 3.  Topical use of Ketoconazole Ointment by myself.  Effect is not obvious. 4.  Diagnosis as epompholyx at a county level hospital. Treatment: Soaking in boric acid solution; oral use of Loratadine and XTR (Xiatare) tablets; Topical use of Urea ointment for one week.  After that change to another antifungal ointment which I don't recall the name. Also prescribed by the physician at the time is Compound Camphor Cream for use when itchy.  With all these, I don't feel itchy at all.  Even if I drink alcohol, the itchiness is still very minimal.  Unfortunately the problem comes back afte 6 months. 5. Soaking in Zuguang Powder+Pikangwang (A chinese herbal medicine containing Ketoconazole) every other night.  Yesterday was the 4 times.  The symptom is relieving a bit.  Skin is shredding, and becoming dry.  Topical use of Pikangwang is not very effective.  Each of the treatment and use of medications mentioned above is spaced at least 2 months apart.  I would be very grateful if you guys can offer any help.  The disease is so troublesome to me. I smoke, and not particularly fond of drinking alcohol (except when I accompany my boss on events).  I am fond of eating spicy food.  However, because of the problem on my hands, I have cut back most of it.  The pictures below are downloaded from the internet.  My situations are quite similar to them.  My skin is thicker, and the area of skin shredding is also bigger as well.  When the weather is warm, I can feel there are numerous blisters below the skin.  It is itchy, more so if I scratch it.
gold_texts: ["Eczema", "Should be eczema at the hands.", "The main thing is to avoid contact with alkaline solutions.  Otherwise problem would be coming back.", "Eczema is very stubborn.  Apart from the use of topical ointment, use of chinese herbal medicine to help conditioning of the body should be considered.", "Oral administration:  Pill prepared from Saposhnikovia divaricata + pill prepared from chinese angelica and Sophora flavescens (or Sophora flavescens tablets).  External use: Soaking in boiled water with 10g each of the following herbal medicines: Sophora flavescens, perfoliate knotweed, Bidens bipinnata, Vitiligo skin, hyssop,  and spina gleditsiae, After that topical use of Shufuda (A chinese over the counter herbal drug to help relieve problem of skin)"]

Last week or two, found some red rashes on his ear and near the eyelash.  A bit rough, with some skin scales.  The edge is quite clear, slightly higher than the skin surface.  The temperature on that part is not conspicuously high. I work in a town-run hospital, have less contact with some diseases and not that knowledgeable.  Would ask for help to see what skin rash it is.  Is is useful to apply vitamin E topically, or use some other drugs.  Thanks a lot.

No treatment needed.", "Use Eloson externally.", "Common disease seen in children.  Diagnosis: seborrheic dermatitis or pityriasis simples.  Either one is possible. Better not to  use hormone based drug externally, such as heparin sodium cream."]

candidate: Possibly tinea cruris.  See dermatologist; inadequate response suggests alternative diagnosis.  Combination therapy acceptable.
query_text: Would expert please help.  Is this tinea cruris? Have been to two hospitals. First one said that it is fungal infection.  Second one said it is tinea cruris. The rashes have come out for almost a month.    Before I went to the hospital, I used ketoconazole ointment topically, and there was seeminly some improvement.  However, the number of rashes were increasing later on.  After I went to the hospital, I started to use bifonazole solution.  But it is not under control.  From the picture, it can be seen the rashes are increasing in size.  With that, I would like expert here to determine whether it is fungal infection or tinea cruris.  If that is the case, why was the medication unable to put it under control, and it is growing and spreading out.  On the surface, there is no leisons, not itchy, not painful.  If that is not the case, what is the problem?  There is no other symptom, no itchiness, no pain, but the number of rashes is increasing and growing bigger.  How long would it take when using antifungal drug to make it effective.  The physician prescribed bifonazole spray and terbinafine hydrochloride cream.  First use the spray and then apply the cream at the problem area.  Is this OK?
gold_texts: ["These two medicines are relatively good to treat tinea cruris.  Should continue to use the drugs for more than 2 weeks, or to continue to use them 1 to 2  weeks after the skin leisons are healing.   This is to avoid recurrence of the disease.", "Suggest to perform a pathological test for fungal infection.  I don't feel it is tinea cruris.", "I don't feel it is tinea cruris.", "Eczema?  Dermatitis?", "Impression: tinea cruris Treatment: terbinafine hydrochloride cream, topical use (must complete the whole treatment cycle)", "Diagnosis to determine to disease:  Should distinguish among erythrasma, seborrheic dermatitis, Proliferative pemphigus,  and Erosive Psoriasis.  5. Treatment:  Obese people should bath regularly and use drying powder to dry up the body.  Treatment should be the same as having tinea corporis.  Because skin at the groin area is very delicate, should not use strong drug to affect the skin.  Generally should use Resorcinol cream or 1% Econazole or Clotrimazole cream, 2 % miconazole, bifonazole, ketoconazole, sertafurazole, butenafine, terbinafine cream are also O.K.   Orally, the use of Flavomycin is rather effective.  But it could easily relapse after stopping the medication.  So it is not recommended. Fluconazol, Itraconazole, Terbinafine etc may be used in short term. Cause of the disease Commonly caused by flocculent epidermal tinea, trichophyton, and red trichophyton.  Sometimes, invasion to the groin area by Candida albicans resulting in red scaling of skin.  May have papulovesicle at the edge.  This disease can happen easily in the humid season, mostly with male who are sweating a lot.  Workers in specific disciplines  such as drivers and people who are in sitting position for extended period of time could contract this disease easily.  2. clinical manifestation Often occurring at the skin opposite to the scrotum, on one side or both.  Mostly exhibiting in a circle or semi-circle rash.  Initially growing as a small patch of rash on the inside of the upper part of the buttock.  There is scaling and spreading to all sides.  The border / edge is rather clear.  On top of it, it has blisters, lumps, and scab.  The central part of it can be healing by itself with scaling and pigment decomposition.  After some time, part of the skin could be thickening and lichenified, along with itchiness.  In serious cases, it can spread to the inner side of the buttock, perineum and its surroundings.  The lower side of rash is rather clear.  Sometimes, it could also affect the scrotum and its root area.  3. Diagnosis:  It can be diagnosed based on clinical manifestation and test on fungal infection.  Diagnosis to determine to disease should distinguish among erythrasma, seborrheic dermatitis, Proliferative pemphigus,  and Erosive Psoriasis.  Definition of Fungus  Fungus is a living creature with real nucleus.  The most common fungus is the mushrooms.  In addition, it also includes Mold and yeast.  There are more than 70,000 kinds of fungus.  It is believed that this only constitutes a small part of the fungus. Originally most fungus is classified as plant or animal. At present, fungus has been classified separately from animal or plant, and forms its own realm in parallel with Animal, Plant, Prokaryotic and Protobiotic Realms. It has a strong cell wall and a real nucleus.  It does not have chlorophyll and is heterotrophic.  Typically, it can be reproduced asexually and sexually, producing spores in various forms.  Based on the characteristics of its growth and difference in physical appearances, it can be separated as yeast, fungus and mushrooms.  Among them, 300 or so could cause diseases. With the exception of Cryptococcus and mushrooms, most of the disease causing fungi are mold medically.  Based on the locations of human body being invaded by fungus, clinically it can be separated into superficial fungal infection and deep fungal infection. Fungal enteritis is a deep fungal infection. Superficial fungal infection invades skin, hair and nail.  Deep fungal infection can invade body skin, mucosa, deep tissues and internal organs, and may result in disseminated hyperinfection of the whole body. Deep fungal infection at the intestines is fungal enteritis. May also appear in babies as candida enteritis, or fungal infection to the whole body, such as Histoplasmosis being a complication with AIDS.", "perform a test on fungal infection.", "Not tinea cruris.  That would be itchy.  Part of the skin mucosa is changing to whitish in color.  Should not appear in the form of rash physiologically.  It looks more like eczema. Recommend to perform a culture test on bacteria.  If it is tinea cruris, may use nystatin orally."]

candidate: Likely infected eczema or contact dermatitis.  Discontinue halcinonide.  See dermatologist.  Avoid picking.
query_text: Sore at the External auditory canal, getting scabbed with discharge. It started first as scoring at the external auditory canal when cleaning / picking the ear.  It was summer back then.  The lesion got wet everyday when bathing, and hence did not heal.  Later on, it gradually deteriorates like oral sores: has discharge, scabs, and with itchiness.  With repeated scratching by fingers, the scab comes off, then starts bleeding.  It has been one year like this, happening recurrently and causing a lot of inconvenience to my daily life.  Have gone to hospital three times, and was given out treatment as Otitis media. It does not help even after spending a few hundred dollars on medications.  After advice from friends, I use Halcinonide Solution.  After using it for 3 to 5 days, the disease got healed.  But it comes back after a week.  With the use of Halcinonide Solution again for half a month, the problem is gone, but relapses a week after stopping the solution.  This repeats again after use of another 2 months.  Now I am at a loss, and don’t know how to deal with it.  Would like to ask experts for help.
gold_texts: ["Eczema?", "Consider: Eczema at the ear?", "infective eczema", "Longdan Xiegan Wan, Longzhu ointment (both are chinese traditional medicines for topical use.)", "Longdan Xiegan Wan, Longzhu ointment (both are chinese traditional medicines for topical use.)"]

Consult a doctor if you have fever, chills, warmth, swelling, pain, redness, bleeding or pus-like discharge.", "Wound appears to be fairly deep ulcer although unclear as to what part of the body or what the initial mechanism of injury was.  Needs additional clinical information to provide further recommendations.  On the singular attached picture there does not appear to be secondary signs of infection.  Will likely need long-term wound care including wet-to-dry dressing changes."]

candidate: This persistent lump could be a granuloma or a foreign body reaction.  Do not attempt to drain it yourself. Please see a physician for evaluation and potential biopsy to determine the cause and appropriate treatment.
query_text: As shown in the picture, finger scratched by a cat. Will it swell into a large lump after the wound heals? A few months ago, my index finger was scratched by a stray cat, breaking the skin and causing a little bleeding. It healed naturally but then formed a swollen lump. I used a needle to puncture it and squeezed out pus, after which the lump flattened. However, it swelled up again shortly after. Now, months later, when I tried using a needle again, nothing came out.
gold_texts: ["The wound appears infected. Seek evaluation in ER to drain abscess and antibiotics.", "I would recommend soaking the area and warm soapy water several times a day to soften the skin and encourage drainage from the wound and help with healing. I would also recommend being seen in person for the incision and drainage as it can be done steriley in the hospital."]

gold_texts: ["Clean with soap and water daily and cover with dry dressing. It will take 1-2 months for the wound to completely heal.", "Blistering suggest a partial-thickness wound.  To promote healing and reduce the risk of infection, recommend applying a topical antibiotic ointment and covering with a nonadherent dressing.  Dressing may be changed daily.  During dressing changes, clean the wound gently with warm soapy water.  Tetanus should be updated if tetanus is not up-to-date."]

For soft tissue injuries that are not amenable to suture repair, to promote wound healing, gently cleanse the wound 1-3 times a day with warm soapy water, avoid any drying agents such as alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, or iodine.  After cleansing wound apply topical antibiotic ointment and cover with a nonadherent dressing.  Tetanus shot should be updated if tetanus is not up-to-date."]

query_text: Will it still grow nail I was slammed by the door pretty hard. There was bruises in the bottom half immediatly. The bruising expanded to everywhere the second and third day. I have clipped the top part of the nail that was separating from the skin. The nail on the side and on the back seems intact with the skin. My nail typically grows very quick, but this have been ten days without growth. Will it stop growing? What should I do.
gold_texts: ["The nail will grow back but it may take up to 6 months.", "There appears to be a underlying hematoma underneath your nail. I would recommend being seen by a physician to have your nail removed and to prevent or inflammation to your nail"]

However, the nail does not grow any more, and the nail is sticking to the blood underneath it, and then the nail had separate from the skin around it. I have clipped the top part of the nail that was separating from the skin. The nail on the side and on the back seems intact with the skin. My nail typically grows very quick, but this have been ten days without growth. Will it stop growing? What should I do.
gold_texts: ["The nail will grow back but it may take up to 6 months.", "There appears to be a underlying hematoma underneath your nail. I would recommend being seen by a physician to have your nail removed and to prevent or inflammation to your nail"]

query_text: Help, I fell from a bike resulting in a wound like this. It has been two days, does it need stitches There was pus formation on the side. The wound is approximately 3-4mm deep. There was occasional bleeding for the past two days. Does it need stitches? If it does not need stitches, will it have hypertrophic scar after the wound heals?
gold_texts: ["Closing the wound at this time risks infection. Continue to cleanse with soap and water and apply dry dressing. Hypertrophic scar formation cannot be predicted at this time but you have a higher chance of developing hypertorphic scar if you have developed them in the past.", "Wound appears to be abrasion with exposed dermis.  Does not appear to be a laceration amenable to suture repair.  If the wound is greater than 24 hours old is not appropriate for suture repair.  To promote wound healing, clean the wound thoroughly with warm soapy water.  You may apply topical antibiotic ointment and cover with a nonadherent dressing.  Dressing may be changed once daily and during dressing changes wound may be cleaned gently with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents such as alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, or iodine to open skin.  Tetanus should be updated if tetanus is not up-to-date.  Once the wound is healed, to minimize scar formation avoid UV exposure and use of scar minimization creams such as Mederma"]

candidate: Superficial wounds typically heal within 1-2 weeks.  Keep the area clean and moisturized.  Peeling is part of the healing process, don't force it. If concerned, consult a physician for personalized advice.
query_text: It's been a week. Does anyone know how long this wound takes to heal? The surrounding area is a bit stiff but not painful. The skin won’t peel off, right?
gold_texts: ["It can take up to 8 weeks for healing. If the skin gets enough blood flow to it and survives, then it will not peel off.", "The margins of the wound are slowly coming together, the skin may peel off however that may be just the dead skin coming off and the underlying skin should be coming together. I would recommend reducing range of motion to your finger as it heals."]

query_text: My finger was hit by the electric fan, and the tissue was everted out I did some compression to push the tissue back inside. The outter layer had grown back together, but a lump had formed, and it is changing color. What should I do, is it infected. Pleas help.

query_text: My wound is infected Picture one is taken right after the fall. The day after the fall, I painted erythromycin ointment at the scarring site, and the ointment might have caused the wound to close. The second image is taken on the fifth day. When I use a q tip to press on the wound, yellow pus will drain out from the wound. gold_texts: ["likely will leave a scar, will take up to 8 weeks to heal", "Fairly large soft tissue injury with scab formation. Would recommend local wound care with topical antibiotic ointment, nonadherent dressing such as Xeroform or Adaptic and changing the dressing daily.  During daily dressing changes, gently cleanse the wound with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents including alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, or iodine."]

query_text: My knee area was caught by the screw on a metal door. The femur was slightly impacted and have a mild dent. How long does it take to heal? The screw was somewhat moist. The wound image below is taken 17 days post the incident. How long will it take to heal? Will I get a scar?

query_text: Fell off my bike a few days ago. Bought ointment and applied it myself. Not sure what to do. Should I wrap it in gauze? I've heard it heals slower with gauze. Also, what medication should I apply?
gold_texts: ["Continue performing wound care with washing with soap and water, applying antibiotic ointment and cover with a bandage. gold_texts: ["这两种药都是治疗股癣比较好的药了  要坚持使用2周以上或是皮损消退在继续使用1--2周以免复发", "去做个真菌刮片吧，感觉不象股癣", "觉得不像股藓", "湿疹？皮炎？", "印象：股癣 治疗：盐酸特比萘芬乳膏 外用（一定要足疗程）", "鉴别诊断 应与红癣、脂溢性皮炎、增殖性天疱疮、糜烂性银屑病等相鉴别。 5治疗 肥胖者应常洗澡并敷以扑粉等。 治疗同体癣。由于阴股部的皮肤较娇嫩，应注意勿用过于**的药水，以免**皮肤，一般用复方间苯二酚涂剂或1%益康唑或克霉唑霜，2%咪康唑、联苯苄唑、酮康唑、舍他糠唑、布替萘芬、特比奈芬霜也可用之。 口服灰黄霉素有一定疗效，但停药后易复发，不宜使用。氟康唑、伊曲康唑、特比奈芬等可短期使用。 病因 常由絮状表皮癣菌、须癣毛癣菌、红色毛癣菌等引起，有时，白色念珠菌也好侵犯腹股沟部位而呈红斑脱屑性斑片，其边缘可有丘疱疹。 本病在温热潮湿的季节容易发生，男性多汗者尤易发病。特殊工种如汽车司机、长期坐位者也易发病。 2临床表现 常发生于阴囊对侧的大腿皮肤，一侧或双侧，多呈环状或半环状斑片。初于股上部内侧出现小片红斑，其上有脱屑，并逐渐扩展而向四周蔓延，边界清楚，其上有丘疹、水疱、结痂。中央部位可自愈，有色素沉着或脱屑，历久则于局部皮肤发生浸润增厚呈苔藓化，常伴痒感。严重者常扩展波及股内侧、会阴或**周围，其下缘多清晰。有时尚可波及阴囊、**根部等处。 3诊断 根据临床表现、皮损形态及真菌学检查可确诊。 鉴别诊断 应与红癣、脂溢性皮炎、增殖性天疱疮、糜烂性银屑病等相鉴别。 5治疗 肥胖者应常洗澡并敷以扑粉等。 治疗同体癣。由于阴股部的皮肤较娇嫩，应注意勿用过于**的药水，以免**皮肤，一般用复方间苯二酚涂剂或1%益康唑或克霉唑霜，2%咪康唑、联苯苄唑、酮康唑、舍他糠唑、布替萘芬、特比奈芬霜也可用之。 口服灰黄霉素有一定疗效，但停药后易复发，不宜使用。氟康唑、伊曲康唑、特比奈芬等可短期使用。 真菌感染定义： 真菌（Fungus）是一种真核生物。最常见的真菌是各类蕈类，另外真菌也包括霉菌和酵母。现在已经发现了七万多种真菌，估计只是所有存在的一小半。大多真菌原先被分为动物或植物。 目前，真菌在分类学上已***为界，与动物界、植物界、原核生物界和原生生物界平行。真菌具有坚固的细胞壁和真正的细胞核，不含叶绿素，是异养性的，以寄生或腐生方式生存，典型者兼有有性生殖和无性生殖，产生各种形态的孢子。根据生长特性与形态差异，可将真菌简单分为酵母、真菌和蕈(蘑菇)。其中对人类有致病性的真菌约有300多个种类。除新型隐球菌和蕈外，医学上有意义的致病性真菌几乎都是霉菌。根据侵犯人体部位的不同，临床上将致病真菌分为浅部真菌和深部真菌。真菌性肠炎即属于深部真菌病。　浅部真菌(癣菌)仅侵犯皮肤、毛发和指(趾)甲，而深部真菌能侵犯人体皮肤、黏膜、深部组织和内脏，甚至引起全身播散性感染。深部真菌感染肠道即表现为真菌性肠炎，可***存在如婴儿念珠菌肠炎，或为全身性真菌感染的表现之一，如艾滋病并发播散性组织胞浆菌病", "做个真菌刮片", "不是股藓，股藓要痒，局部皮肤粘膜有白色粘膜样改变，一般不会出现疹子一样的病理，倒像是湿疹，做个细菌培养看看，如果是股藓可以配合制霉菌素口服。"]

query_text: 关于湿疹、汗疱疹，求诊断 手上湿疹、汗疱疹从10年至今，反复发作，痛苦啊，望各路医生给看看。以下是记录： 工作历程：09年6月毕业，之后工作，因工作需要接触低浓度盐酸，盐酸在槽内，用来洗去金属表面铁锈，操作过程无手套等保护，直接接触盐酸，同时双手参与体力活动，经常直接接触工业油污。10年3月后换单位，一直在办公室，偶尔接 触金属，不好判断是否金属过敏引起。 病症：双手手掌侧皮层内有泡，很痒，抓破后流出谈黄色液体，越抓越痒，皮损后结壳，皮肤变硬、脱屑。夏天多发、冬季减缓。 用药记录： 1、地级市皮肤医院诊断为湿疹、汗疱疹，用药醋酸铝粉末冲调后泡后，外用曲咪新乳膏，用后效果不明显。 2、自用药水杨酸浸泡，效果不明显，类似用药记录5。 3、自用复方酮康唑软膏，效果不明显。 4、县级市一医院诊断汗疱疹，用药硼酸浸泡、内服氯雷他定和百癣夏塔热片、外用尿素软膏一周后改用一种软膏，具体忘记了，好像是抗真菌的，同时医生开有复方樟脑乳膏，说痒的时候用。这一套下来之后不痒了，连喝酒之后都只有轻微的痒。不过半年后复发。 5、自用足光散+皮康王。足光散浸泡间隔一晚一次，昨天浸泡第4次。浸泡后痒的症状减轻了许多，手上脱皮、干燥，外用皮康王。效果感觉也不是很理想。 以上用药记录至少互相间隔2各月以上。各位大神，有没有好点的方法，跪求了。这病好烦人哎。 本人抽烟，不喜酒（陪领导的时候是没办法），爱吃各种**、辛辣，因为双手这样，也忌口了很多，哎。 以下图片是网上找到的，跟我手上差不多，我手上厚皮、脱屑面积比他大一些，天热的时候摸明显摸到皮下有很多泡，有点痒，越抓越痒的那种。

query_text: 关于湿疹、汗疱疹，求诊断 手上湿疹、汗疱疹从10年至今，反复发作，痛苦啊，望各路医生给看看。以下是记录： 工作历程：09年6月毕业，之后工作，因工作需要接触低浓度盐酸，盐酸在槽内，用来洗去金属表面铁锈，操作过程无手套等保护，直接接触盐酸，同时双手参与体力活动，经常直接接触工业油污。10年3月后换单位，一直在办公室，偶尔接 触金属，不好判断是否金属过敏引起。 病症：双手手掌侧皮层内有泡，很痒，抓破后流出谈黄色液体，越抓越痒，皮损后结壳，皮肤变硬、脱屑。夏天多发、冬季减缓。 用药记录： 1、地级市皮肤医院诊断为湿疹、汗疱疹，用药醋酸铝粉末冲调后泡后，外用曲咪新乳膏，用后效果不明显。 2、自用药水杨酸浸泡，效果不明显，类似用药记录5。 3、自用复方酮康唑软膏，效果不明显。 4、县级市一医院诊断汗疱疹，用药硼酸浸泡、内服氯雷他定和百癣夏塔热片、外用尿素软膏一周后改用一种软膏，具体忘记了，好像是抗真菌的，同时医生开有复方樟脑乳膏，说痒的时候用。这一套下来之后不痒了，连喝酒之后都只有轻微的痒。不过半年后复发。 5、自用足光散+皮康王。足光散浸泡间隔一晚一次，昨天浸泡第4次。浸泡后痒的症状减轻了许多，手上脱皮、干燥，外用皮康王。效果感觉也不是很理想。 以上用药记录至少互相间隔2各月以上。各位大神，有没有好点的方法，跪求了。这病好烦人哎。 本人抽烟，不喜酒（陪领导的时候是没办法），爱吃各种**、辛辣，因为双手这样，也忌口了很多，哎。 以下图片是网上找到的，跟我手上差不多，我手上厚皮、脱屑面积比他大一些，天热的时候摸明显摸到皮下有很多泡，有点痒，越抓越痒的那种。
